long_description: Adapter Kind: Adapter Instance: vCD - dirw082.us-south.vmware.cloud.ibm.com - Collecting - Data receiving VMWARE - w082-vc.w082.dalpr.dir - Collecting - No data receiving pSAN - w082-vc.w082.dalpr.dir - Collecting - Data receiving vRealizeOpsMgrAPI (vRealize Operations Manager Collector-vrops-master) - Collecting - Data receiving Infrastructure Health Adapter - vRealize Operations Collector-vrops-data04 - Collecting - Data receiving VMWARE - w082-vc.w082.dalpr.dir - vSAN - Collecting - Data receiving Alert Description: The host has been unexpectedly disconnected from vCenter Server. The virtual machines on the host have also lost connectivity to vCenter Server. The host might not have a problem and the virtual machines on the host might be running as expected, but any configuration, data, and events from the host and its virtual machines are lost. Alert ID: 58c2fcf6-1e7e-4296-8020-22e401c781ec Alert Impact: health Alert Name: Host has lost connection to vCenter Server - IBM Alert Subtype: Availability Alert Type: Virtualization/Hypervisor Cancel Date: Unavailable Control State: Open Criticality: critical Host Name: vrops-ui.dalpr.dir Alert Link: https://vrops-ui.dalpr.dir/vcf-operations/ui/inventory;mode=hierarchy;resourceId=802524a0-ef38-4e84-84c2-aabf503b6876;tab=alerts-and-symptoms;subTab=alerts;entityId=58c2fcf6-1e7e-4296-8020-22e401c781ec Notification Rule Description: Notification Rule Name: PD: Host has lost connection to vCenter Server Object Efficiency State: -1.0 Object Health State: 4.0 Object Risk State: 2.0 Product: ['N/A', 'N/A', 'N/A'] Recommendations: Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev1_alerts.html#host-has-lost-connection-to-vcenter-server | Click "Open Host in vSphere Web Client" in the Actions menu at the top of Alert details page to connect to the vCenter managing this host and manually reconnect the host to vCenter Server. After the connection to the host is restored by vCenter Server, the alert will be canceled. | If you added this host to a different vCenter, remove it from the current vCenter. Runbook URL: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev1_alerts.html#host-has-lost-connection-to-vcenter-server Resource ID: 802524a0-ef38-4e84-84c2-aabf503b6876 Resource Kind: HostSystem Resource Name: w082d13esx014.w082.dalpr.dir Status: Active Symptoms: Symptom Set - self Symptoms Host disconnected from vCenter | w082d13esx014.w082.dalpr.dir | 802524a0-ef38-4e84-84c2-aabf503b6876 : notResponding != connected Recommendations: Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev1_alerts.html#host-has-lost-connection-to-vcenter-server | Click "Open Host in vSphere Web Client" in the Actions menu at the top of Alert details page to connect to the vCenter managing this host and manually reconnect the host to vCenter Server. After the connection to the host is restored by vCenter Server, the alert will be canceled. | If you added this host to a different vCenter, remove it from the current vCenter.
